articles.title: Freshpet Boosts Full-Year Outlook But Investors Frown Over EBITDA Margin Decline
articles.description: Freshpet, Inc. (NASDAQ: FRPT ) reported first-quarter results on Wednesday that beat Wall Street expectations and raised its full-year sales outlook, driven by double-digit volume growth and margin expansion. Despite the strong results and higher guidance, Freshpet shares traded lower following the earnings release. Freshpet reported first-quarter EPS of 91 cents, beating analyst estimates of 15 cents. Net sales increased 13.1% year over year to $297.6 million, above estimates of $291.8 million . Net income totaled $48.5 million, compared with a net loss of $12.7 million a year earlier. Growth, Margins Improve Sales growth was driven by volume gains of 14.6%, partly offset by unfavorable price and mix of 1.5%. Gross profit increased to $120.7 million from $103.8 million, while gross margin expanded to 40.5% from 39.4%. Adjusted gross margin rose to 46.9% from 45.7%, helped by lower input costs and improved plant leverage. Operating income totaled $4.3 million, compared with an operating loss of $11.5 million in the ... Full story available on Benzinga.com

articles.title: An update on the Boost Nomination Program, and what’s next
articles.description: Plus details about how stories will continue to be Boosted When we launched the Boost Nomination Program, we wanted to work with those closest to the writing on Medium: publication editors. We partnered with a small group of editors in a limited capacity to help us find and develop stories that could be Boosted. We learned a lot from working closely with those editors, and we appreciated their contributions. However, as time went on, we found the program couldn’t scale, and it limited the kinds of stories we could reward editors for, so we’ll be closing the Boost Nomination Program on May 31st, 2026. How will stories now get Boosted? As a writer, you won’t notice a difference in how stories are Boosted. The way we review stories for Boost is staying the same , and we’ll continue applying the same Distribution guidelines to stories . The publications you know and love working with are already one of our main sources for finding stories to Boost, so your best bet remains submitting your story to a high quality publication and working with an editor to polish your story. In 2023, we added a human level of how we recommend stories to readers — by Boosting them. Boosted stories are more likely to bring new members to Medium, and we frequently hear from readers that they pay for Medium because of the consistently high quality of stories found here. We’ll continue to review stories for Boost at the same rate across Medium , because it remains a great way to discover and reward some of the best stories on Medium. What’s next? We learned so much from the editors in the Boost Nomination Program — especially that we gain a lot when we work closely with Medium editors. We want to be much more expansive in how we reward editors for the work they do to develop writers and stories, elevate stories to their communities, and how well they understand their publication’s readership here. That’s why we’re excited to launch the Editor Partner Program we announced earlier this week. This new program will reward qualifying editors for all stories they work on, regardless of whether or not they meet Boost criteria. We’re looking forward to learning more from our early participants, and expanding the program in July to bring in more eligible publications. An update on the Boost Nomination Program, and what’s next was originally published in The Medium Blog on Medium, where people are continuing the conversation by highlighting and responding to this story.
